title: Error-TIMESCALEMOD on not-found-modules due to non-"generate"ed instances
body:
From https://github.com/cocotb/cocotb/issues/2471#issuecomment-799909496.

## Summary

`Error-TIMESCALEMOD` being reported when multiple Verilog sources are being compiled with the `--timescale` option set. None of the Verilog files have a timescale directive.

## Tried

* `--timescale-override`. Got the same error
* adding timescale directives to each file. Got the same error
* switching file order. Error changed to point to second file in list.
* removing the `--timescale` option. Everything works.

Also noteworthy is that cocotb uses `--timescale` in it's regression with no issues. My best guess is because there is only one SV file in each test.

Failing test is in https://github.com/jg-fossh/Goldschmidt_Integer_Divider/tree/main/sim. You can run it by installing cocotb `pip install cocotb` and issuing `make SIM=verilator`. The commands it uses are in the following output dump if you don't want to install and use cocotb.

```
verilator -cc --exe -Mdir sim_build -DCOCOTB_SIM=1 --top-module GID_TOP --vpi --public-flat-rw --prefix Vtop -o GID_TOP -LDFLAGS "-Wl,-rpath,/home/kaleb/dev/cocotb/cocotb/libs -L/home/kaleb/dev/cocotb/cocotb/libs -lcocotbvpi_verilator" --trace --trace-structs --timescale 1ns/1ps /home/kaleb/dev/Goldschmidt_Integer_Divider/sim/../source/Goldschmidt_Integer_Divider_2CPS.v ./GID_TOP.v /home/kaleb/dev/cocotb/cocotb/share/lib/verilator/verilator.cpp
%Error-TIMESCALEMOD: /home/kaleb/dev/Goldschmidt_Integer_Divider/sim/../source/Goldschmidt_Integer_Divider_2CPS.v:368:8: Timescale missing on this module as other modules have it (IEEE 1800-2017 3.14.2.2)
                     /home/kaleb/dev/Goldschmidt_Integer_Divider/sim/../source/Goldschmidt_Integer_Divider_2CPS.v:58:8: ... Location of module with timescale
   58 | module Goldschmidt_Integer_Divider_2CPS #(
      |        ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
        ... See the manual and https://verilator.org for more assistance.
```

Known to affect v4.106 and current stable (v4.200).
This may be related https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/2763#issuecomment-792495487.
